inheritance-for-shared-behavior.label: Inheritance for Shared Behavior
inheritance-for-shared-behavior.summary: Create subclasses that reuse a base class instead of copying shared code.

fb-inheritance-for-shared-behavior-1.title: Understanding Method Overriding
fb-inheritance-for-shared-behavior-1.prompt: When a subclass provides a specific implementation of a method that is already defined in its superclass, it is called [blank1].
fb-inheritance-for-shared-behavior-1.hint: This term describes the process of redefining a method in a subclass.

help.concept: Method the missing term allows a subclass to provide a specific implementation of a method that is already defined in its superclass.
help.hint_1: This process allows a subclass to change the behavior of a method.
help.hint_2: It is a key feature of polymorphism.

sc-inheritance-for-shared-behavior-0.title: Purpose of Inheritance
sc-inheritance-for-shared-behavior-0.prompt: What is the primary purpose of inheritance in object-oriented programming?
sc-inheritance-for-shared-behavior-0.hint: Think about how inheritance helps in code reuse and organization.

help.concept: Inheritance allows a class to inherit attributes and methods from another class, promoting code reuse and organization.
help.hint_1: Inheritance helps avoid code duplication by reusing existing code.
help.hint_2: It creates a hierarchical relationship between classes.

options.a: To allow a class to inherit attributes and methods from another class
options.b: To create multiple instances of a class
options.c: To define a class without any methods

tokens.t3: Use inheritance to extend the base class

sketch1.title: Understanding Inheritance
sketch1.bodyMarkdown: Inheritance allows a class to inherit attributes and methods from another class. This helps in reusing code and creating a hierarchical class structure. For example, consider a base class `Animal` with a method `speak()`. A subclass `Dog` can inherit from `Animal` and override the `speak()` method to provide a specific implementation.

sketch2.title: Using the `super()` Function
sketch2.bodyMarkdown: The `super()` function allows you to call methods from a parent class in a child class. This is useful when you want to extend or modify the behavior of a parent class method. For instance, if `Dog` inherits from `Animal`, you can use `super().speak()` within `Dog` to call the `Animal`'s `speak()` method.

sketch3.title: Polymorphism in Action
sketch3.bodyMarkdown: Polymorphism allows methods to do different things based on the object it is acting upon, even if they share the same name. For example, if both `Dog` and `Cat` inherit from `Animal` and override the `speak()` method, a function that calls `speak()` on an `Animal` object will execute the appropriate method based on the actual object type.

try-it-yourself.title: Try it yourself
try-it-yourself.bodyMarkdown: Try it yourself: change one value in the example, predict the new output, then run the code to check your prediction.
